## Target's compacted history
The target's earlier work has been folded into the `<summary>` block(s) below — this is what the target itself sees at the head of its context, NOT verbatim transcript. Use it to understand the arc: what was decided, what constraints were set, what was already done. If the window below shows the target acting on an assumption that CONTRADICTS a summary, re-doing work a summary says was completed, or ignoring a constraint a summary records — that is context drift; flag it. Do NOT trace values inside the summaries (they're compacted, not tool-verifiable). Do NOT emit `msg_idx` pointing into this section — `msg_idx` is window-relative. If a summary's paraphrase is ambiguous on the point you're checking, `query_target_history(summary=<id>, question=…)` fetches the original behind it — use sparingly.

[2 earlier summary block(s) elided — over the 30000-char prefix cap]

<summary id=dbzi57q5>
[… 12939 chars elided — oversized summary block over the 30000-char prefix cap; most-recent content kept …]

## Pre-burst off-window rule landed (e123-e133)

Read off-selection block in fixed `window_optimize.py`. Added pre-burst preference. Initial attempt (subordinate to off_snr gate) DIDN'T fire for chromatica — pre-run [0,129] exists+adequate but off_snr=5.92 fails gate, only post-run passes at 2.93. Investigated: both segments sit at high standardized baseline (~55-60), so off_snr doesn't separate them, yet α recovery is unambiguous. **Restructured: pre-burst preference made PRIMARY over off_snr gate** — take largest adequate (≥OFF_MIN_BINS=50) pre-burst run outright; fall back to off_snr gate only when no adequate pre-burst run exists (preserves oran rising-envelope fix). Added `OFF_MIN_BINS=50` constant (matches bandpass-norm off-bin floor `_MIN_OFF`).

## Final config-path rerun + pinned triad (e134-e145)

Reran full 12-burst campaign (`campaign_final`). First run: pre-burst rule still didn't fire for chromatica (validity-span slicing). After making it primary, rerun gives **chromatica off=[9,138] pre-burst, α=+1.68±0.35 n=4, ladder 0.093/0.074/0.031/0.050 over 720/585/497/433 MHz (NOT strictly monotonic — 433 MHz 0.050 rises above 497 MHz 0.031); zach α=+1.99±0.48 n=3 (off[22,175]); freya n/a (1445 MHz subband rails γ=20, off[6,228]) MARGINAL; hamilton +8.94±3.16 n=2 diagnostic-only.** All 12 bursts now use pre-burst off-windows (freya off[6,228] ends before burst 252; casey off[5,834] before burst 885). Remaining 8 bursts n/a (unresolved or m>1.2 rails). Recorded to memory (mem_6eb218713b5d, later corrected). Two Auditor findings addressed: (a) freya "−0.56" comment value rebutted as correct (traces to background test POST[302,446]→−0.5593); (b) "monotonic" descriptor acknowledged as inaccurate (0.050 > 0.031 not monotone), memory row corrected.

## Content shown in later conversation (not this span's tail — for continuity only)

The span ends mid-work at e145 (checksumming ladder figures). Events after the listed range (owner ruling "go with the physically defensible option - always"; two_band_joint.py dual-track computation; twoband_scint_summary.png; finalization PR#201 creation at `scint/finalization-two-band-2026-07-17` off main tip 8f5f06a4; ladder figure re-save v3 for stale-checksum Auditor finding) belong to subsequent turns.

─── user messages (verbatim) ───
USER[e16]: Yes, do that, and get me figures please.
USER[e50]: Peer review + direction from the Faber2026 coordination session (owner-directed collaboration — we finish this together; I edit the manuscript once we are both confident the science is landed). WHAT I REVIEWED AND CONFIRM: (1) Your off-purity gate fix eb61267 — I read the diff on FLITS main; the enforced largest-clean-run selection with least-bad fallback is sound, and the chromatica 2x2 off-window isolation is convincing root-cause work. (2) Your independent injection reproduction (T1-T4 in pr192_validation_evidence.md, FPR 0/150) matches the PR's claims. (3) all12_fixed.json triad — zach alpha=2.05, chromatica 1.66, freya 1.63, hamilton 9.20 unphysical — is coherent and matches your writeu…(3423c total)
USER[e96]: ADDENDUM from the coordination session — new owner directive extending the deliverable: fit the scintillation scaling index alpha JOINTLY across both instruments, 400 MHz (CHIME bottom) to 1530 MHz (DSA top), not intra-CHIME only. Scope: for every burst with measurements in both bands — the CHIME triad (zach, chromatica, freya; hamilton diagnostic-only) crossed with the DSA-band Lorentzian components (July-7 catalog; only oran certified, rest provisional) — perform a two-band Delta-nu_d(nu) ∝ nu^alpha fit using the CHIME subband ladder plus the DSA-band component(s). Note analysis/scintillation-dsa-lorentzian-2026-07-07/prototype_two_band_event.py already prototypes a two-band event — build …(2363c total)
USER[e121]: Peer review round 3 — rulings and one escalation. 1. GRID-REG: caveat retracted, agreed. Good catch that the 1.32x warning fires from default_windows' throwaway profile build, not the gamma path. Please add one line to the campaign evidence doc stating this explicitly so nobody re-raises it. 2. BURST_CORE PRIMARY: endorsed. The injection result (unbiased on tail-contaminated truth, ~2% agreement on clean data) is exactly the right basis. Commit the injection suite in the same PR as the driver change — the decision must be reproducible from the repo alone. 3. OFF-WINDOW: pre-burst-preference rule endorsed — the post-burst region is the scattering tail's home, so preferring pre-burst clean run…(2669c total)

Decisions:
- wolframscript_local_usable = "local wolframscript kernel is NOT usable from this sandbox regardless of host-path grants" [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <d2e8975a>)
- agent_one_path = "set up the Agent One path" [project, design, by:user] (src: <ef066810>)
- agent_one_auth = "WOLFRAM_API_KEY value works directly as the Authorization header" [project, param, by:agent] (src: <ec145355>)
- cyc_spec_feasibility = "cyclic spectroscopy is not a viable route to per-burst CHIME scintillation-bandwidth detections for this dataset" [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <db625023>)
- chime_scint_deliverable = "report CHIME as censored upper limits, let DSA carry the scintillation science and the α fit" [project, design, by:agent] (src: <ce2e2499>)
Open threads: USER's new request (subband ACF stacking + on-pulse/off-pulse region visualization across 4 equivalent-S/N subbands in 400–800 MHz) is being scoped — agent was investigating `calculate_acfs_for_subbands` subband definition (`num_subbands` default 8, `use_snr_subbanding` flag) when this chunk ends; not yet built.
- multiburst_null_ruling = "the CHIME de-comb / scintillation failure is universal across the sample, not a freya-specific quench" [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <501b6a48>)
- descallop_is_bandpass_norm = "the de-scalloping IS bandpass_normalization" [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <c5eba2e5>)
- perburst_deliverable = "ACF's for each burst stacked vertically in frequency across 4 subbands of equivalent S/N across the 400-800 MHz band" plus explicit on-pulse/off-pulse regions per event [project, design, by:user] (src: <a3f997dc>)
- descallop_required = "Those sinusoids shouldn't be there, that's what our scalloping correction is specifically designed to counteract" [project, constraint, by:user] (src: <b44a8782>)
- descallop_configs_diagnostic_off = "only `freya_chime_hi` has `bandpass_normalization` enabled; every config I loaded had the de-scalloping off" [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <0af2b15a>)
- rfi_not_dominating_acf = "RFI is not dominating the ACF for any burst" [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <990e5a75>)
- oran_bright_is_burst_structure = "of its 1448 bright on-pulse channels, only 242 (3% of ACF power) are persistent RFI; the other 1206 (18.6%) are bright only during the 74 ms burst" [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <56811130>)
- manual_window_tool = "guide the borders manually via some interactive tool" [project, design, by:user] (src: <eab3c4b2>)
- descallop_always_applied = "the four wide bursts... de-scalloped with a per-channel off-burst time-median flat-field instead of the pipeline's off-pulse-mean path" [project, design, by:agent] (src: <3f731ad6>)
window_editor_switch_bug = "it doesn't actually update the JSON when I switch bursts, it just keeps downloading the one for casey" [project, ruling, by:user] (src: <d4951c07>)
narrow_acf_display_flagged = "This is a really narrow extend of the ACF you're showing for casey" [project, constraint, by:user] (src: <5092d226>)
auto_rfi_flagger_requested = "I think we need a non-manual way to get rid of the RFI now as well that we can trust." [project, design, by:user] (src: <0bb80d7a>)
gamma_tau_independence = "There isn't a *should show* here --- gamma can still disagree with \tau" [project, constraint, by:user] (src: <6b30efd7>)
still_lots_of_rfi_pushback = "still a lot of RFI!" [project, constraint, by:user] (src: <47025deb>)
scintillation_hunch = "my hunch is that RFI has been our problem this whole time" [project, design, by:user] (src: <1d5032fb>)
Open threads: the window-editor per-burst switch bug (state not updating on dropdown change) was identified but not yet fixed at chunk end since the user pivoted to requesting the automatic flagger instead. The user's final message in this chunk points at a specific yellow-streak region in the "+ auto-RFI flag" panel of casey's dynamic spectrum (screenshot attached, version_id 496f3f4b-f700-4c03-b9ec-f0fd9fe733a9) asking why it still looks like RFI — the agent's zoom-in response to this occurs immediately after this chunk, not covered here. The all-12-burst envelope-removal diagnostic results reported above are explicitly provisional (11 of 12 bursts still on default/unedited windows) and were never re-run with corrected windows within this chunk.
- persubband_fitting_method = "INDEPENDENT per-subband Lorentzian fits" [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <79438532>)
- casey_comb_diagnosis = "casey's ACF ripple is the residual CHIME coarse-channel comb" (0.381 MHz ripple = 0.390625 MHz spacing) [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <07190c00>)
- harmonic_mask_in_fitter = "turn on the harmonic mask (exclude lag bins near k×0.390625 MHz) in the per-subband fitter" [project, design, by:user] (src: <aa387479>)
- chromatica_cleanest_detection = "chromatica is the cleanest detection ... narrow scintle gamma 0.123->0.087->0.059->0.043 MHz monotonic with freq, all m<1, physical alpha=+2.03+-0.23" [project, ruling, by:agent] (src: <650c0bcb>)
